avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Main accumulations of deepest recent snow will be on North West through North to South East aspects above 800m. The snowpack in all locations will be wet or moist depending on altitude with wet snow instability on some very steep slopes and gullies above 950m. All cornices will be weak and prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Dry for most of the period but some very light rain is possible at the very end of the day. Slightly cooler in the early part of the evening but noticeably milder temperatures at all altitudes for the remainder of the forecast period.
Observed Weather Influences: Overnight snow showers died out leaving a dry day. South West winds eased to moderate. Cold at all levels overnight then milder from dawn onwards.
ObservedSnowStability: Period of poor stability on steep North West through North to South East aspects above 900m. New weak cornices are present above all recent very steep lee slopes. Better stability on less steep ground at lower altitudes. The avalanche hazard is Considerable

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will slowly consolidate as milder conditions take effect but pockets of windslab will persist. Greatest instabilities will be mainly on North-West through North to East aspects above 900 meters. Cornices above similar aspects will become unstable and prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Overnight frost possible but the freezing level will generally be well above the summits. A mainly dry day with some light rain at the end of the period. Winds will generally be strong South-Easterly.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry day with the freezing level around 1000 metres. Winds were strong South-Westerly.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable windslab exists the greatest accumulations are in wind sheltered areas mainly above 900 metres particularly in sheltered gullies and on steep slopes with a NW, N, NE, E and SE aspect. Fragile cornices exist above similar aspects. Cross loading may affect other wind sheltered locations. Wind exposed slopes are firm and often icy with better stability.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will slowly consolidate in the mild conditions. However, wet snow instabilities will be present on steep slopes mainly on North-West through North to East aspects above 1000 metres. Cornices will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will be mainly dry with some rain late in the afternoon. Winds will be around gale force. The freezing level will be above the summits.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a dry day with very strong South-South-Westerly winds. After a cold start the freezing level rose to around summit level in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of unstable windslab persist mainly on North-West through North to East aspects above 1000 metres. Elsewhere the snowpack is slowly consolidating and stability is better. Cornices are prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Snow surface layers will soften, especially where exposed to direct sunlight. The vast majority of snow will be generally well bonded. Minor surface wet snow instability will be displayed by recent deposits in steeper wind sheltered locations on North through East to South-East aspects above 1000 metres. Collapsing cornice remnants on these aspects will provide the greatest hazard in the very mild conditions. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: No precipitation has been forecast. Strong South-East or South-South-Easterly winds. Freezing level rising up to 2750 metres during the evening with the air temperature gradually rising to reach plus 8 degrees Centigrade at 900 metres by Tuesday afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: No precipitation. Moderate Westerly winds, backing and increasing to become strong Southerly. Freezing level rising to around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Recent windslab accumulations are consolidating with the vast majority becoming generally well bonded. Moderately bonded windslab persists in the deeper accumulations that can be found in steeper wind sheltered locations on North through East to South-East aspects above 1000 metres.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will thaw and consolidate in the mild temperatures. Any instabilities that remain in deeper deposits on North-East to South-East aspects will consolidate through the day. Surface instabilities possible on steep slopes exposed to the sun. Any remaining cornices will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A very mild day with strong South-Easterly winds. Dry for most of the day with rain developing towards the end of the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry day with increasing South-South-Easterly winds. The freezing level rose above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is gradually consolidating. However instabilities remain in recent windslab deposits. Steep North-East to South-East aspects above 750 metres are most affected. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: A steady thaw at all altitudes; however, there will be potential for redistribution of lying snow. Unstable windslab will be present on North-West through North to South-East aspects above 900 metres. Areas most affected will be in steep locations such as gully exits, coire rims and convexities. Remaining cornices will become progressively unstable, potentially triggering slopes below.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It is expected to be dry throughout the forecast period. Strong Southerly winds will increase to gale force and back to become South-South-Easterly. Turning milder during the night as the freezing level rises to well above the summits.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry day with fresh South-Westerly winds. The freezing level rose to 1000 metres by midday.
ObservedSnowStability: Widespread accumulations of unstable windslab are present on North-East to South-East aspects above 900 metres. These accumulations are more localised on North-West to North aspects and on steep banks and burn lines below 900 metres. On these aspects cornices are fragile. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
